skill.name: interface-design
skill.description: Guides the design of dashboards, admin panels, SaaS apps, tools, settings pages, and data-heavy product interfaces.
skill.summary: Design polished product interfaces with stronger systems, hierarchy, and intent.
skill.author: dammyjay93

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from markdown fences, inline code, CSS examples, slash command names, and design prose. No prompt injection, network access, credential access, shell execution, or cryptographic implementation evidence was found. The only confirmed concern is low-risk project-local memory guidance to read and optionally write .interface-design/system.md.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick execution findings are not confirmed. The cited content is markdown syntax, inline code formatting, CSS examples, slash command names, and interface design prose rather than executable code or malicious instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain documentation and example text, not runnable source code. No evidence found for actual shell execution, cryptographic use, reconnaissance behavior, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Project-Local Design Memory File Access
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read .interface-design/system.md when present and optionally write design patterns there after asking the user. This is scoped to a project-local design memory file and shows no exfiltration, but it can persist interface decisions in the repository.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly reference reading and updating a named project file. The risk remains low because the file is local, user-facing, and there is no hidden execution or network transfer.

content.value_statement: Generic dashboards and admin panels often feel templated and inconsistent. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create product interfaces with clearer intent, systems, hierarchy, and polish.
actual_capabilities: Frames interface work around product context, user goals, and visual intent.
Guides dashboard, admin panel, SaaS app, settings, and data interface design.
Defines surface, border, typography, spacing, color, depth, and interaction principles.
Encourages custom controls, complete states, and consistent component patterns.
Provides validation checks for hierarchy, signature elements, tokens, and system reuse.
limitations: It is not intended for landing pages, marketing sites, or campaign design.
It provides design guidance, not a complete UI component library.
It may ask for clarification before building when product intent is unclear.
It can reference a local design memory file only when the project uses one.

use_cases.title: Design a SaaS Dashboard
use_cases.description: Create a dashboard layout with meaningful hierarchy, product-specific visuals, and reusable interface patterns.

use_cases.title: Refine an Admin Panel
use_cases.description: Improve spacing, navigation, controls, states, and surface treatment in a dense operational tool.

use_cases.title: Audit Interface Craft
use_cases.description: Review an existing product screen for generic patterns, weak hierarchy, inconsistent tokens, and missing interaction states.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Product Screen
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new interface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the interface-design skill to design a dashboard for [product]. Focus on the user, their main task, and a clear visual direction.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the interface-design skill to review this screen and improve hierarchy, spacing, color, controls, and states without changing the product goal.

prompt_templates.title: Create a System Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the interface-design skill to define the interface direction, surface system, spacing scale, typography hierarchy, and component patterns for this product.

prompt_templates.title: Audit for Generic Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the interface-design skill to audit this interface for default dashboard patterns, weak signature elements, token drift, and missing state coverage.

output_examples.input: Design a dashboard for a deployment monitoring tool.
output_examples.output: A focused operations interface with dense status rows, quiet borders, monospace deployment data, clear failure states, and navigation built around incident response.

output_examples.input: Improve a settings page for a team management app.
output_examples.output: A reorganized settings surface with clearer grouping, consistent form controls, visible disabled and focus states, and a calmer hierarchy for repeated admin work.

output_examples.input: Audit this analytics screen for craft.
output_examples.output: A concise review identifying generic metric cards, inconsistent spacing, weak chart hierarchy, missing loading states, and specific changes to make the screen feel product-specific.

best_practices: Start with the product domain, target user, core task, and intended feeling before choosing visuals.
Use a consistent system for surfaces, borders, typography, spacing, color, depth, and states.
Run the swap, squint, signature, and token checks before presenting interface work.
anti_patterns: Using standard dashboard cards, sidebars, and metric boxes without product-specific intent.
Mixing shadows, borders, spacing values, radii, and colors without a declared system.
Treating native controls, empty states, loading states, and focus states as unfinished details.

faq.question: What type of work is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for product interfaces such as dashboards, admin panels, SaaS apps, tools, settings pages, and data-heavy screens.

faq.question: Is this skill for landing pages?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly excludes landing pages, marketing sites, and campaign design.

faq.question: Does it generate production code?
faq.answer: It guides interface design decisions and can support implementation, but it is primarily a design process skill.

faq.question: Can it help with existing interfaces?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review screens for hierarchy, spacing, tokens, component consistency, states, and generic design patterns.

faq.question: Does it store project memory?
faq.answer: It can suggest saving reusable patterns to .interface-design/system.md when the user agrees.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
